title: tripuls

gpu_support.notes: GPU inputs are gathered to the host and evaluated with the scalar reference implementation.

fusion.notes: Fusion is disabled because width and skew validation are handled by the host builtin.

description: `tripuls(T)` returns samples of a unit-width triangular pulse centered at zero. `tripuls(T, W, S)` uses width `W` and skew `S` in `[-1, 1]`.
behaviors: Operates element-wise on real numeric scalars, vectors, matrices, and N-D tensors; preserves input shape.
The pulse is zero outside `[-W/2, W/2]` and reaches one at `S*W/2`.
The width must be positive and finite.
The skew must be finite and in `[-1, 1]`.
Complex and text inputs are rejected with a builtin-scoped error.

faqs.question: Can `tripuls` be used inside `pulstran`?
faqs.answer: Yes. `pulstran(T, D, 'tripuls', W, S)` evaluates triangular pulses centered at each delay.
